title: AP 物理C力学 2025年样题1
description: AP AP 物理C力学 2025年样题1

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

questions.prompt: A conservative net force Fx is exerted on an object within a system. The force varies as a function of the position x of the object, as shown in the graph. There are no net external forces exerted on the system. Which of the following correctly describes the potential energy Usys of the system as the object moves from 0m to 0.10m?

options.content: Usys increases only.
Usys decreases only.
Usys decreases and then increases.
Usys does not change.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
A constant force is exerted on a block, causing the block to move across a horizontal surface in the positive x-direction. There is nonnegligible friction between the block and the horizontal surface. The diagram shows the position of the block at 0.5s time intervals. Which of the following provides a velocity-time graph that could correspond to the diagram and provides correct reasoning?

Velocity-Time GraphReasoning
AThe distance traveled by the block during each half-second interval increases, so the velocity of the block must increase.
BThe block is traveling in the positive x-direction, so the velocity graph must have a positive slope.
CThe force of friction exerted on the block causes the acceleration of the block to decrease.
DThe block reaches a constant speed.

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Two disks, A and B, rotate for 4 seconds. Each of their angular velocities, ω, are measured over time and graphed in the figure. Which of the following correctly compares the angular displacements ΔθA and ΔθB of disks A and B, respectively, during the period shown, and provides a correct reasoning?

options.content: ΔθA=ΔθB because Disk A has a greater angular velocity at the end of the time interval shown.
ΔθA=ΔθB because neither curve has a decreasing slope.
ΔθA≠ΔθB because Disk B stops rotating after 1 second.
ΔθA≠ΔθB because the area under Disk A’s curve is greater than the area under Disk B’s curve.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Block X of mass 3.0kg and Block Y of mass 5.0kg are connected by a string and are on a level horizontal surface. There is negligible friction between the blocks and the surface. A force F=12N is exerted on Block Y, as shown. What is the tension in the string connecting the two blocks?

options.content: 1.5N
3.0N
4.5N
7.5N

questions.prompt:
Student 1 is standing on a cart holding a small stone, while Student 2 is standing at rest on the ground, as shown. The cart is moving at a constant speed v in the +x-direction, as indicated by the coordinate system. Student 1 drops the stone just as they pass Student 2. Which of the following best represents the path of the falling stone observed by Student 1 and the path of the falling stone observed by Student 2 ?

Path Observed by Student 1Path Observed by Student 2
A
B
C
D

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.prompt:
A physical pendulum is constructed of a small block of mass 2m attached to the end of a uniform rod of length ℓ and mass m. The rotational inertia of the rod about its end is 13mℓ2. The rod is pulled back a small angle θ and released at time t=0, as shown. The rod is vertical at time tv.

Which of the following is a correct expression for tv?

options.content: tv=π22ℓ15g
tv=π214ℓ15g
tv=π2ℓg
tv=π214ℓ5g

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
A physical pendulum is constructed of a small block of mass 2m attached to the end of a uniform rod of length ℓ and mass m. The rotational inertia of the rod about its end is 13mℓ2. The rod is pulled back a small angle θ and released at time t=0, as shown. The rod is vertical at time tv.

Which of the following correctly justifies why the motion can be called simple harmonic motion?

options.content: The torque is directly proportional to the sine of the angle that the pendulum makes with the vertical, regardless of how large the angle is.
The pendulum is released at a small angle, so the torque is directly proportional to the angle that the pendulum makes with the vertical.
The torque is directly proportional to how far the end of the pendulum is from its equilibrium position, regardless of how far from equilibrium it is.
The torque is directly proportional to the length of the pendulum.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: A small sphere of mass m is attached at the right end of a rigid rod of length ℓ. The mass of the rod is negligible compared to m. The rod is free to rotate about a pivot at its left end, as shown. The rod-sphere system has rotational inertia I about the pivot. By what factor should the length of the rod be increased to increase the rotational inertia of the rod-sphere system to 2I?

options.content: 12
12
2
2

questions.prompt: Spheres X, Y, and Z have the masses and locations indicated in the figure. What is the magnitude of the net gravitational force on Sphere X due to the other two spheres?

options.content: 2Gm29r2
Gm2r2
11Gm29r2
3Gm22r2

questions.prompt: Two stones are thrown from the same height and with the same initial speed. Stone A is thrown vertically downward, and Stone B is thrown horizontally, as shown. If the stones are thrown at the same time and air resistance is negligible, which of the following is true?

options.content: The two stones reach the ground at the same time with the same speed.
The two stones reach the ground at the same time but with different speeds.
Stone A reaches the ground first, but the two stones have the same speed just before they hit the ground.
Stone A reaches the ground first and has greater speed just before hitting the ground.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Four satellites A, B, C, and D have circular orbits about Planets X and Y, as shown. Planet Y has twice the mass of Planet X. Satellite A has mass m and orbits a distance R from the center of Planet X. Satellites B and C have masses m and 2m, respectively, and orbit a distance 2R from the center of Planet X. Satellite D has mass m and orbits Planet Y in a distance 2R from the center of the Planet X. Which of the following correctly ranks the orbital periods TA, TB, TC, and TD of satellites A, B, C, and D, respectively, from least to greatest?

options.content: TA<TD<(TB=TC)
TA<(TB=TC=TD)
TD<TA<(TB=TC)
TD<TA<TB<TC

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Cart A of mass 2m is moving with constant speed v to the right on a horizontal track. Cart B of mass m is initially at rest, as shown. The carts collide elastically. Which of the following best describes the motion of the carts immediately after the collision?

options.content: Cart A is moving to the left and Cart B is moving to the right.
Cart A is stationary and Cart B is moving to the right.
Both carts are moving to the right at the same speed.
Both carts are moving to the right, but they travel at different speeds.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: A block of mass m is attached to one end of a spring with the other end of the spring attached to a wall. The block slides with negligible friction on a horizontal surface, as shown. When the block is at position x=x0, the block has speed v=v0 and the total energy of the block-spring system is E0. When the block reaches position x=2x0, the block momentarily comes to rest. Which of the following correctly represents the total energy of the block-spring system when the block reaches position x=2x0?

options.content: 0
E0
2E0
4E0

questions.prompt: An object is moving along the y-axis. The object’s position as a function of time is given by y=αt3−βt+ϕ, where α=1m/s3, β=4m/s, and ϕ=5m. What is the acceleration of the object at time t=3.0s?

options.content: 6m/s2
18m/s2
20m/s2
23m/s2

questions.prompt: A nonideal spring is fixed to a wall. A small bumper on the end of the spring is initially at position x=0 when the spring is at its equilibrium length, as shown. When the spring is compressed a distance x from equilibrium, the elastic potential energy Us of the spring in joules is given by Us=3x3−4x2+2x. What is the spring force Fs as a function of x?

options.content: Fs=9x2−8x+2
Fs=−9x2+8x−2
Fs=34x4−43x3+x2
Fs=−34x4+43x3−x2

questions.prompt: A narrow hole is drilled from the surface of a spherical asteroid to its center. The asteroid has a mass M and radius R. A small object is dropped into the hole. What is the acceleration of the object when it reaches a distance R3 from the center of the asteroid?

options.content: 19GMR2
13GMR2
3GMR2
9GMR2

questions.prompt: A sphere is held at rest at the top of a ramp, as shown. The sphere is released and rolls without slipping down the ramp. The gravitational potential energy of the sphere-Earth system is zero at the bottom of the ramp. Which of the following is true of the sphere when it reaches the bottom of the ramp?

options.content: The sphere’s rotational kinetic energy equals the initial potential energy of the sphere-Earth system.
The sphere’s translational kinetic energy equals the initial potential energy of the sphere-Earth system.
The sphere’s translational kinetic energy and rotational kinetic energy are equal.
The sum of the sphere’s translational kinetic energy and rotational kinetic energy equals the initial potential energy of the sphere-Earth system.

questions.prompt: A board of length L and mass M is horizontally suspended from strings A and B which are attached to the left and right ends of the board, respectively. A small block of mass 2M is attached to the board a distance x from the left end of the board, as shown. At time t=0, String B is cut. What is the angular acceleration of the board immediately after t=0? The rotational inertia of the board rotating about its edge is 13ML2.

options.content: 32gL
g(L+2x)13L2+2x2
g(12L+x)13L2+x2
g(12L+2x)13L2+2x2

questions.prompt: Two small blocks A and B have masses m and 2m, respectively. Both blocks are released from the same location and slide down an incline with negligible friction. Both blocks travel a vertical distance Δh before smoothly transitioning to a horizontal surface. The coefficients of kinetic friction between the horizontal surface and blocks A and B are μA and μB, respectively, where 2μA=μB. Block A travels a distance D before coming to rest on the horizontal surface. What is the distance Block B travels along the horizontal surface before coming to rest?

options.content: 14D
12D
2D
4D

questions.prompt: The work done on an object is described by the equation W=3t2+5t−1, where W is in joules and t is in seconds. What is the power delivered to the object at t=2.0s?

options.content: 10.5W
16.0W
17.0W
21.0W

questions.prompt: Two cars A and B are driven on a section of road that has a circular curve. When both cars are driven through the curve at their maximum speeds before they slip on the road, the centripetal acceleration of Car B is twice that of Car A. The maximum speed Car A can drive through the curve without slipping is 10m/s. Which of the following is closest to the maximum speed Car B can drive through the curve without slipping?

options.content: 7.0m/s
10m/s
14m/s
20m/s

questions.prompt: A box of mass m is pulled across a horizontal surface by a rope. The rope exerts a variable force Frope directed at an angle θ from the horizontal that is described by the function Frope=cos(βx), where β is a positive constant. The box is pulled from position x=0 to x=D, where Frope<mg. Which of the following is the work done on the box by the rope as it is pulled from x=0 to x=D?

options.content: Dcos(βD)
Dcos(βD)cosθ
βsin(βD)cosθ
1βsin(βD)cosθ

questions.prompt: A block on a horizontal surface is attached to one end of a horizontal spring. The other end of the spring is attached to a wall. The block oscillates with amplitude A and period T, as shown. There is negligible friction between the block and surface. At time t=0 the net force on the block is zero. Which of the following is an expression for the speed of the block at time t=T6?

options.content: v=πAT
v=33AT
v=π3AT
v=2π2A3T

questions.prompt: A 2.0kg block is attached to a string that passes over a pulley and is attached to an ideal spring of spring constant k=100N/m, as shown. The pulley and string have negligible mass, and there is negligible friction in the pulley. The block is held in place with the spring at its original unstretched length and then released from rest. Which of the following is approximately equal to the amplitude of the resulting motion?

options.content: 0.02m
0.10m
0.20m
0.40m

questions.prompt: A wedge of mass M is balanced about its center with a small cylinder of mass m attached to its narrowest end, as shown. The linear mass per length of the wedge can be modeled by an equation of the form: λ=bx2, where b is a constant with appropriate units and x is the distance from the narrowest end. What is the ratio Mm of the masses of the two objects?

options.content: 23
1
2
3

questions.prompt: While a satellite orbits the Earth at a constant distance of one Earth radius R above Earth’s surface, the total energy of the satellite-Earth system is ER. When the satellite is moved to a circular orbit a distance 3R above Earth’s surface, the satellite-Earth system has a total energy E3R. Which of the following correctly relates E3R to ER?

options.content: E3R=13ER
E3R=12ER
E3R=2ER
E3R=3ER

questions.prompt:
Two identical carts A and B are on a horizontal surface with negligible friction. Cart A approaches Cart B, which is initially at rest. Attached to Cart B is a spring that is initially compressed, as shown in Figure 1. At the instant Cart A collides with Cart B, the spring is released, and the carts move away from each other, as shown in Figure 2. Which of the following correctly compares the kinetic energy and the momentum of the two-cart-spring system after the collision to those quantities before the collision?

Kinetic EnergyMagnitude of Momentum
AIncreasesDecreases
BIncreasesStays the same
CStays the sameIncreases
DStays the sameStays the same

options.content: A
B
C
D

ω(t)=9t12−2
ω(t)=6t12−2
ω(t)=92t−12

5000N

questions.prompt: The work required to accelerate an object from 10m/s to 20m/s is WA. The work required to accelerate the object from 20m/s to 30m/s is WB. Which of the following correctly compares WA to WB?

options.content: WA<WB
WA=WB
WA>WB
The mass of the object must be known to compare the relative magnitudes of WA and WB.

40J

questions.prompt: A torsion spring is fixed to the end of a rod of rotational inertia IR. The torsion spring is fixed to a horizontal table with negligible friction, as shown in the Top View. When the rod is displaced an angle θ from equilibrium, the torsion spring exerts a restoring torque of magnitude τ=2κθ2, where κ is a positive constant with appropriate units. After being displaced by an angle θ0, the rod is released and rotates through its equilibrium position with angular speed ω0. Which of the following is a correct expression for ω0?

options.content: Zero
2κθ033IR
2κθ03IR
2κθ033IR

questions.prompt:
Two pucks A and B have masses m and 3m, respectively. The initial speed of Puck B is vB. The pucks move toward each other in the xy-plane, collide, stick together, and then move in the +y-direction. There is zero net external force on the two-puck system. The paths and directions of the pucks before and after the collision are shown in the diagram.

Which of the following expressions best represents the speed of the two pucks after the collision?

options.content: 12vB
34vB
vB
32vB

questions.prompt:
Two pucks A and B have masses m and 3m, respectively. The initial speed of Puck B is vB. The pucks move toward each other in the xy-plane, collide, stick together, and then move in the +y-direction. There is zero net external force on the two-puck system. The paths and directions of the pucks before and after the collision are shown in the diagram.

The collision is repeated, but Puck A is replaced with Puck C, which has half the mass and twice the speed of Puck A in the original collision. Pucks B and C stick together after the collision. Which of the following best describes how the direction of pucks B and C compares with the direction of pucks A and B after the original collision, and provides an appropriate justification?

Direction of Pucks B and C after CollisionJustification
ASame direction as Pucks A and BThe initial momentum of Puck C is the same as the initial momentum of Puck A
BSame direction as Pucks A and BBoth collisions are inelastic.
CDifferent direction than Pucks A and BThe speed of Puck C is greater than the speed of Puck A
DDifferent direction than Pucks A and BThe total mass of the two-puck systems is different in each collision.

options.content: A
B
C
D

questions.prompt: The magnitude of the momentum of an object as a function of time, in seconds, is given by p=(4kg⋅m/s3)t2. The magnitudes of the net force exerted on the object at times t1 and t2=2t1 are F1 and F2, respectively. Which of the following correctly compares F2 and F1 ?

options.content: F2=2F1
F2=4F1
F2=8F1
F2=16F1

questions.prompt:
A block on an incline is initially at rest. There is nonnegligible friction between the block and the incline. In Scenario 1, the block is given a brief push at time t=0, and begins to slide down the incline with speed v0. In Scenario 2, the block is placed at the same initial position, is given a brief push at time t=0, and begins to slide up the incline with initial speed v0, as shown. The direction down the ramp corresponds to positive velocity. Which of the following pairs of velocity-time graphs could correctly represent the motion of the block in Scenario 1 and Scenario 2 ?

Scenario 1:Block Travels Down InclineScenario 2:Block Travels Up Incline
A
B
C
D

options.content: A
B
C
D
